Re: Request by Banning City Councilmember, Richard Garcia, for
Letter Opinion Relative to Potential Conflict of Interest
Arising From Participation in the Voting on Matters Pertaining
to the Sun Lakes Village Housing Development Where the Memlcer
Resides
I am currently a first-term member of the City of Banning City
Council . f have over two years remaining on my term.
I reside in Sun Lakes Vil1age, which is an age-restricted community
within the City. 
The project will ultimately contain some 4,000
dwelling units of various types. Approximately 1,300 units have
been completed. The project developer has come before the City
Council with requests for approval of Tentative Tract Maps and
Amendments of the specific Plan and Development Agreement which are
in place between the developer and the City.
The existing and proposed residential portions of the project are
subject to a Mello-Roos Assessment District. 
The assessment is
currently conatructed in such a way that individual property owners
pay an assessment on their residence. The developer currently Pays
the assessment for that portion of the project on which residences
have not yet been constructed.
The assessrnent currently rises at the rate of 4t annua1Iy. My
assessment for '91-92' was $208.10. My assessment for '92-93' will
be $216.42. The potential conflict arises because of a provision
in the Mello-Roos document which provides that following the sale

sections.question_synthetic: Richard Garcia, a Banning City Councilmember residing in Sun Lakes Village, seeks clarification on whether he has a conflict of interest that would prevent him from voting on matters related to the Sun Lakes Village Housing Development project, given that he receives a minor financial benefit from Mello-Roos assessment changes at project buildout.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from the client (Richard Garcia) to the FPPC, not an FPPC response letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are marked in the document. The request is embedded in narrative form within the body of the letter. Date is June 11, 1992. Addressed to Mr. Wallace at FPPC.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Richard Garcia, a Banning City Councilmember residing in Sun Lakes Village, seeks clarification on whether he has a conflict of interest that would prevent him from voting on matters related to the Sun Lakes Village Housing Development project, given that he receives a minor financial benefit from Mello-Roos assessment changes at project buildout.

embedding.summary: Richard Garcia, a Banning City Councilmember, requests a letter opinion from the FPPC regarding a potential conflict of interest arising from his residence in Sun Lakes Village and his participation in City Council votes on the developer's requests for approvals, where he would receive a minimal financial benefit (less than $30/year) from assessment reductions at project buildout.
